title: Connect subitems to another board

posts.author: leo3 (leo3)
posts.cookedHtml:
hello
I am trying to link subitems with another board .
I create the column “connect boards” and then try to connect to the subitems of a board but it is not indicated in the possible selection however sometimes I have the possibility to select the board “Subitems of board X” … why is it possible sometimes and why sometimes not .
How to make it possible to link subitems of board X with another board Y

Translated with www.DeepL.com/Translator (free version

here i do not se here it is posible |690x337, 100% e subitem boards1172×1424 71.9 KB
![here it is posible |690x337](upload://yaJgetz9X87iaSJG2oSSOPH04FH.png)

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @leo3!

At this time, when connecting subitems to other boards, it is only possible to set up the connection from the subitem itself. As you have you have experienced, it is not currently possible to choose subitems from a connect boards column, unless the connect boards column was initially set up in the subitems themselves - does this make sense?

If you’re experiencing questionable behaviour, please reach out to our support via the help center so they can investigate this for you

posts.author: leo3 (leo3)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Bianca for your help

i tried creating a "connected boards " from the subitems but that doesn’t work either . really annoying
I actually want to create a template from that board … but several automations and especially integrations don’t work when you create a new board from that template
Really disappointing .
I hope to find a solution for this for my company

leo

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sorry to hear this Leo… if this behaviour doesn’t seem expected please reach out to our support team as our technical specialists will be able to investigate

Regarding automations/integrations on template boards, all automations that are turned on will duplicate over to the templated board. In regard to custom recipes using apps and integrations at this time these are NOT supported in the board duplication/templating process. These recipes will not duplicate over. (ie: Gmail / Outlook/ Slack/ Apps). I apologise for the setback here

Please cast your vote on the existing feature request to put greater emphasis on this need and get this on our developers radar!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @leo3 - Are you trying to connect subitem to subitem or subitem to an item?

posts.author: Christoph (Christoph)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any workaround to connect the subitem to an existing connect boards column in the other board?

We built an advanced time tracking setup in Monday, where we have one time tracking board for each customer / project and then mirror all entries to one overview board which we use for reporting. This works better for us than a Dashboard. But now I have a project, where I need to do the time tracking in subitems whereas in the other boards the time tracking was done on item level. All other boards are connected to the same connect boards column in the reporting board. Now I need to connect the subitems from the new project to that same column.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to connect subitem to subitem. Is there such an option?

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: @Christoph @Soroh try the app “Same Item Multiple Boards” - it allows you to sync subitems from an item in board 1 to an item in board 2. It’s an alternative to connected boards/mirror columns. You can take a look here: monday.com: Apps Marketplace

posts.author: Christoph (Christoph)
posts.cookedHtml:
@garfield-labs indeed I found that app recently and it looks promising. However, the issue wer are facing with marketplace apps is that we need to sign a Data privacy agreement (we want our data to be safe and also be compliant with EU data privacy regulations) with the respective providers. So far, we didn’t get a satisfying DPA from any provider yet.

The developer’s website of this app does not even show an imprint. That doesn’t make us confident to trust them with our data and it doesn’t look promising to get into discussions about a DPA if the website is even lacking such basic information.

posts.author: b_rad (Brad Jerger)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice to be able to connect sub items to other boards in the same manner you can connect items so you can mirror their info. Regardless if on the connected board it pulled into a item or sub-item

posts.author: Sara413 (Sara Mahan)
posts.cookedHtml:
@garfield-labs Is it possible for the subitems to live in multiple boards under a different parent items?
Scenario:
Board A - Projects Listed at parent level, assignments at a subitem level which include estimated time/effort spent on each task
Board B - Employee directory, Employees listed at the parent item level

Ideally, I would like a way to have the assignments live under the project on the project board but under the employee on the directory for capacity planning.

We use the workload widget for resourcing projects from day to day/week to week but we are looking for a way to determine annual capacity needs.

Thanks

posts.author: Max_Poltor (Max Poltor)
posts.cookedHtml: It seems to be quite logical to be able to see and choose items and their corresponding subitems in connected boards column.

posts.author: Max_Poltor (Max Poltor)
posts.cookedHtml: In fact, the search by subitem keywords picks up an item of subitem, which indicates that they are still linked, but simply not visible.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

We had a similar challenge in our workflow, when we needed to quickly connect items and keep the communication clear. What helped our team was trying out marketplace add-ons that simplify task creation and management.

For example, we use Capture (to turn annotated screenshots directly into tasks) and Email to Tasks (to convert emails into actionable monday.com items). Both were originally built for our own internal needs, but over time we released them publicly since they solved real problems for us.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Soroh ,

This is possible, but it takes a few steps to get there. Let me show you.

You have Board ABC and Board XYZ, and you want to connect to each others’ subitems.

  1. Go to Board ABC.
  2. Go to any subitem or create a fake subitem.
  3. On that subitem, add a connect board column connecting to Board XYZ with two-way connection.
  4. Go to Board XYZ.
  5. Go to any subitem or create a fake subitem.
  6. Add a column on the subitem level by clicking Copy parent columns and selecting the newly connect board column from subitems Board ABC.

Now your subitems Board ABC are connected to subitems Board XYZ.

Feel free to delete the connect board column on the item level of Board XYZ if you’d like. It isn’t needed anymore.

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Christoph @Soroh , happy to inform you that everything you mentioned is actually possible!

With Connected Boards Automations, you can set up exactly what you need in literally 4 minutes or less. Check out this 4-minute video tutorial and follow the steps.

–> How to connect an item from the Main Board to multiple subitems in different boards

@Soroh in your case, you just have add an additional step. As David mentioned above, you need to use the “Copy parent columns” function in the board where the “link to Subitems of Board” connect column was created.

Also, if you want to sync data from your item in the Main Board to all the connected subitems, you can use any of these cool automations from the Connected Boards Automations app as well. Two-way syncing is also possible!

image2364×726 42.2 KB

image2202×770 39.1 KB

Hope this helps! Feel free to email us anytime if you need help! We’d be happy to hop on a quick call with you if you need assistance.
